message:
perf(combinator): replace O(n) remove with tombstone GC in RateLimiter wait queue

Apply the same tombstone-and-GC pattern used in the Bulkhead refactor
to the RateLimiter's VecDeque-based wait queue:

- Add RejectionReason::Consumed variant as the tombstone marker
- check_entry(): Mark consumed entries with Consumed instead of removing
  via VecDeque::remove(idx), converting from O(n) shift to O(1) mark
- cancel_entry(): Read entry fields before tombstoning, then drop the
  lock before refunding tokens, avoiding holding the queue lock during
  token state mutation
- process_queue(): GC tombstones from the front before timeout scanning
- Handle double-poll of Consumed entries (return Cancelled)

This mirrors the Bulkhead optimization and ensures consistent O(1)
amortized dequeue cost across both queue-based combinators.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
